business_overview.description: BG operates in the agricultural sector with principal segments including crop production and processing. The company's geographic footprint spans North America, South America, and EMEA, serving key end markets such as food, feed, and industrial applications.

extraction_notes: Company name is not explicitly stated in the provided text.

risk_factors_top.title: Adverse weather conditions
risk_factors_top.summary: Adverse weather, including climate change impacts, can affect agricultural commodity availability and pricing, as well as operations and supply chains.

risk_factors_top.title: Ongoing war between Russia and Ukraine
risk_factors_top.summary: The war may cause property damage, inventory loss, business disruption, and expropriation of assets in Ukraine. It also increases cybersecurity risks and insurance coverage uncertainties.

risk_factors_top.title: Commodity price volatility
risk_factors_top.summary: Prices for agricultural commodities are volatile due to supply and demand factors beyond the company's control, affecting operating results and profitability.

risk_factors_top.title: Economic and geopolitical risks
risk_factors_top.summary: Global economic uncertainties, including China's economic slowdown and international trade disputes, pose significant risks to the company’s operations and financial performance.

risk_factors_top.title: Regulatory and legal risks
risk_factors_top.summary: The company is exposed to various regulatory and legal risks such as trade policies, environmental regulations, tax laws, and antitrust concerns that could impact its business strategies and profitability.

risk_factors_top.title: Operational risks in international markets
risk_factors_top.summary: Operating internationally involves numerous operational challenges including enforcement of agreements, currency exchange rate fluctuations, and compliance with diverse legal requirements.

risk_factors_top.title: Acquisition and divestiture risks
risk_factors_top.summary: The company faces uncertainties related to the success of acquisitions, joint ventures, and divestitures, including integration challenges, potential liabilities, and regulatory hurdles.

risk_factors_top.title: Regulatory compliance
risk_factors_top.summary: The company faces risks related to various business regulations, including taxes, accounting, anti-corruption, trade sanctions, product safety, environmental matters, and facility licensing. Compliance issues could result in fines, administrative sanctions, criminal penalties, shutdowns, and other liabilities.

risk_factors_top.title: Environmental and operational risks
risk_factors_top.summary: The company is exposed to environmental liabilities from past operations, potential accidents that can lead to personal injuries or facility shutdowns, and costs associated with remediation. Regulatory restrictions on land use and water use also pose challenges.

risk_factors_top.title: Credit risk
risk_factors_top.summary: The company is exposed to credit risks from customers and suppliers, particularly in Brazil where it provides financing to farmers. Non-payment or defaults could significantly impact financial results.

risk_factors_top.title: Cybersecurity risks
risk_factors_top.summary: The company faces cybersecurity risks due to potential cyberattacks, especially in regions affected by political tensions and military conflicts. The company has implemented security measures but cannot guarantee their effectiveness.

risk_factors_top.title: Operational risks from third-party dependencies
risk_factors_top.summary: Third-party suppliers and partners are critical to the company's operations, and disruptions in their performance could lead to manufacturing challenges, shipment delays, increased costs, or lost revenue. The company has taken steps to mitigate these risks but cannot guarantee full reliability.

risk_factors_top.title: Public health crises
risk_factors_top.summary: The company's operations can be adversely affected by public health crises, pandemics, and epidemics due to their impact on employee health, facility operations, supply chain disruptions, and customer base. The extent of the impact is difficult to predict.

risk_factors_top.title: Executive management risks
risk_factors_top.summary: The company's success depends heavily on its executive management team and key personnel. The loss of any one or more of these individuals could significantly impact operations, and Swiss law restrictions may make it challenging to recruit for certain positions.

risk_factors_top.title: Legal risks related to data privacy
risk_factors_top.summary: The company is subject to evolving data privacy regulations that could lead to legal claims or proceedings, regulatory fines, and damage to reputation if non-compliance occurs. The company has implemented measures but cannot guarantee full compliance.

risk_factors_top.title: Shareholder rights and capital structure
risk_factors_top.summary: Anti-takeover provisions in Articles of Association may limit shareholder influence and ability to obtain a premium for shares.

risk_factors_top.title: Acquisition risks
risk_factors_top.summary: Pending acquisition with Viterra poses uncertainties, including potential termination, regulatory delays, and impact on business relationships.

risk_factors_top.title: Debt financing risks
risk_factors_top.summary: The company may incur significant debt to fund an acquisition, which could impact its financial position and increase costs.

segments.name: Ag Services and Oilseeds
key_drivers: Strong demand for agricultural inputs and services
key_concerns: Adverse weather conditions impacting crop yields
products_services: Agricultural inputs, crop protection products, seed treatments, fertilizer application services

segments.name: Agribusiness
key_drivers: Lower average sales prices experienced in all regions for our global soybean oilseed processing businesses
Higher volumes in South America resulting from the non-recurrence of the prior year drought in Argentina
key_concerns: Overall lower volumes in our global soybean oilseed processing businesses
Lower average sales prices in our global corn, wheat, and oil businesses

segments.name: Refined and Specialty Oils
key_drivers: Increased volumes in Asia due to higher demand for certain products driven by better pricing
Expanded capacity at our Avondale refinery
key_concerns: Uncertainty related to U.S. biofuel policies
Devaluation of the Egyptian pound in the first quarter of 2024

segments.name: Milling
key_concerns: Lower sales prices in both our South American wheat milling and North American corn milling businesses

key_drivers: decreased Net sales in the current period driven by factors described in the Segment Overview & Results of Operations section above
